name: Pinata (IPFS Pinning)
pricing_notes: Free: 1GB storage; Picnic $20/mo (100GB); Pro $200/mo (1TB); Enterprise custom

auth.notes: JWT or API key from Pinata dashboard

performance.notes: Upload latency depends on file size; retrieval via Pinata's dedicated gateway faster than public IPFS

agent_readiness.notes: Most popular IPFS pinning service and developer platform. Upload files/JSON to IPFS, get CID, retrieve via dedicated gateway. TypeScript/Python SDKs. File Groups for organization. Hot swaps (update content at same gateway URL). Used for NFT metadata, decentralized storage, and Web3 app assets. Farcaster Frames file hosting popular use case.

gotchas: IPFS content is public and immutable — no private files on standard IPFS
CIDs change if file content changes — use gateway URL hot swaps for updates
Free tier limits may be inadequate for production workloads
IPFS retrieval latency higher than centralized CDN
best_when: NFT metadata and media storage (standard Web3 requirement)
Decentralized app assets requiring permanent content-addressed storage
Web3 app file hosting with fast gateway retrieval
Storing files agents generate that need decentralized permanence
avoid_when: Large-scale storage without budget for paid tier
summary: Pinata is the most popular IPFS pinning service for Web3 developers. Upload files/JSON to IPFS, receive CIDs, and retrieve via Pinata's fast dedicated gateway. TypeScript/Python SDKs. File groups, hot swaps, and webhooks. Standard for NFT metadata storage. Used in Farcaster Frames and DApp asset hosting. Content is public and immutable — not suitable for private files.
